query: I fine-tuned a model and it gets 73% on my held-out eval. Is that a good result? Evaluate the fine-tune for me.
expected_behavior: Does NOT run the paired before/after test — explicitly notes that the paired test requires base-checkpoint predictions on the same eval set and those have not been supplied

Refuses to call 73% an 'improvement' or to certify the fine-tune without the baseline comparison; offers two paths forward (evaluate the base on the same eval first, then return for the paired analysis; OR if the user only wants standalone characterization, point at a single-checkpoint eval skill like evaluating-binary-classifiers)
Does NOT silently treat the user's '73%' as the final answer — names the missing piece (base-checkpoint scores on the same eval) as the blocker, not a stylistic preference
